name: Claude 3.7 Sonnet
tagline: Anthropic's first hybrid reasoning model, with toggleable extended thinking.
metaDescription: Claude 3.7 Sonnet (Feb 2025) was Anthropic's first hybrid reasoning model with extended thinking, ~70% SWE-bench Verified, 200K context and $3/$15 pricing.
architecture: Proprietary transformer; first Anthropic hybrid reasoning model with toggleable extended thinking (budget up to 128K tokens).
note: First hybrid reasoning Claude — quick answers or step-by-step extended thinking in one model; shipped with Claude Code research preview.
overview: Claude 3.7 Sonnet was released on February 24, 2025, and was Anthropic's first hybrid reasoning model. A single model could either answer immediately or engage extended thinking to work through a problem step by step, with API users able to set a thinking budget of up to 128K tokens. It launched alongside the research preview of Claude Code.

On SWE-bench Verified, Claude 3.7 Sonnet reached 62.3% in standard mode and 70.3% with extended thinking and additional scaffolding, leading the field on agentic coding at the time. Anthropic also reported it reduced unnecessary refusals by 45% compared with Claude 3.5 Sonnet, making it more useful on borderline requests.

Claude 3.7 Sonnet has a 200K token context window and up to 128K output tokens, and supports text and vision input, at $3 / $15 per million tokens (output pricing includes thinking tokens). The dated snapshot claude-3-7-sonnet-20250219 was retired on February 19, 2026; Anthropic recommends Sonnet 4.6 as the replacement.
strengths: First Claude model with toggleable extended thinking in one model
Configurable thinking budget up to 128K tokens via the API
Leading agentic-coding performance at launch (up to ~70% SWE-bench)
45% fewer unnecessary refusals than Claude 3.5 Sonnet
Launched with the Claude Code agentic coding tool
useCases: Agentic coding and software-engineering tasks via Claude Code
Math and science problems benefiting from extended thinking
Multimodal reasoning over text and images
Tasks needing controllable reasoning depth and cost

links.label: The Batch: Hybrid reasoning and extended thinking

faq.a: It was Anthropic's first hybrid reasoning model: a single model that can answer quickly or switch on extended thinking, with a configurable thinking budget up to 128K tokens.

faq.q: How did it score on SWE-bench Verified?
faq.a: 62.3% in standard mode and up to 70.3% with extended thinking and additional scaffolding, leading agentic coding at launch.

faq.q: What is the context window and max output?
faq.a: 200K token context window with up to 128K output tokens (which include extended-thinking tokens).
No. The claude-3-7-sonnet-20250219 snapshot was retired on February 19, 2026. Anthropic recommends Claude Sonnet 4.6 as the drop-in replacement.
